name: Mexican Guacamole Dip
directions: Split avocado in half.
Remove pit.
Score avocado meat with knife vertically and then horizontally.
This will create little cubes of avocado.
Repeat with remaining halves, place in a bowl larger enought to make dip.
Add diced tomatoes, onions, and minced fresh cilantro.
Mix all ingredients together.
Mix until ingredients form a dip consistency.
Dip should have some lumps.
Add salt to taste.
Mix.
Serve with chips, tacos, or just eat plain out of the bowl!

name: Super Easy & Tasty Guacamole
directions: Remove the avocado from its skin and start mashing with a fork in a bowl.
Sprinkle with desired amount of garlic salt and pepper.
Add desired amount of salsa (I prefer La Victoria Salsa Suprema medium).
I guarantee this guac will taste great, and you can mix it up in about 1 minute.
Enjoy.

name: Guacamole
directions: Cut avocadoes in half lenghthwise, remove seeds and scoop avocado out of peeling and into a medium sized mixing bowl.
Add all other ingredients into bowl and mash with a fork or potato masher until smooth and well blended.
Chill and serve.

name: No-Cilantro Guacamole
directions: Slice avacodos in 1/2, remove seeds.
Remove flesh into a bowl.
Add garlic.
Add tomatoes.
Add lime juice.
Add onion.
Add salt.
Mash all together with a fork.
Open bag of Tostitos corn tortilla chips or "scoops".
ENJOY!
Adjust seasonings to taste.  I don't measure when I make this I just "dump" and go.
You may reserve the seed(s) and place in bowl AFTER mixed.  Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refridgerate if not using immediately.  The seeds help keep the avacodo from turning brown.

name: Guacamole Pronto!
directions: Mash avocadoes with fork or potato masher.  Add picante to taste and salt and pepper.

name: Da Best Guacamole!
directions: Cut avocadoes, remove pit and scoop out --place into medium sized bowl.
Mash avocado well with fork.
Add remaining ingredients and mix well.
Serve chilled with Tortilla chips.
Enjoy!
Note: This dip is better chunky, so resist the temptation of sticking everything in a food processor!

name: Guacamole
directions: Peel, pit and mash avocados.
In a medium size bowl combine avocados with tomatoes, chilies, onion, garlic and cilantro.
Season with salt and pepper and squeeze lime juice over top.
***Bury avocado pits (I used 3) in guacamole to prevent darkening--but remove before serving***.
Garnish with cilantro sprigs if desired.

name: Homemade Guacamole
directions: Cut avocados in half and remove the seed.
In blender or food processor place all ingredients.
Blend until desired consistency.

name: Salsa/Pico De Gallo II
directions: Put everything in a food processor. Pulse 10 times or until desired consistency. Chill for a couple of hours or until the next day. Enjoy!

name: Guacamole
directions: In a small bowl, combine the tomato, onion, cilantro, jalapeno, garlic powder, salt and lime juice; set aside.

Carefully cut avocados in half lengthways; remove pit with a spoon.  Scoop out the avocado pulp and mash coarsely with a fork.  Toss mashed avocado with tomato mixture.  Taste and adjust seasoning.  Serve immediately with baked tortilla chips or use as a garnish with your favorite Mexican recipes.

name: Easy Guacamole
directions: Peel and de-pit the avocados.
Mash all ingredients together in a bowl using a fork until it is a smooth paste.
That's it!
If you are going to grill the avocados first, cut them in half length-wise and remove the pit.  (Try planting it in a pot, the tree makes a cute plant).
Place them peel side down on a preheated BBQ grill or in a toaster oven until just heated through.
Follow the recipe directions as above.

name: Best Guacamole Ever!!!!
directions: Combine Avocado, Garlic Cloves, and Onion.
Add lime juice, but taste as you add it --  the one thing that could potentially kill this recipe is too much lime.
Add salt and pepper to taste (you can add this before the lime juice too).

name: Family Favorite Guacamole
directions: Chop cilantro coarsely, place in medium bowl.
Cut jalapeño pepper in half (remove and discard seeds); chop finely and add to bowl.
Chop onions finely and add to bowl.
*The trick to making this the best guacamole ever is to take the cilantro, jalapeno, and onion and put it into a food processor (I own the bullet which makes it so easy!) to make it almost pureed.

Cut avocados into quarters; then cut into bite-size pieces. (Score the inside of the avocados in a crisscross pattern through the flesh, just down to the skin. Flatten skin slightly and slide knife across the bottom, next to the skin, to remove the cubes.) Add avocado pieces to bowl.
Add salt, pepper, and cumin and stir into mixture until blended.
Squeeze juice of one-half lime (about 2 teaspoons) into mixture; mix gently until blended.

name: Melt in Your Mouth Guacamole
directions: Mash avocados so that it is of a smooth consistency, and then mix in the cream cheese.
Add the rest of the ingredients.
Chill or enjoy immediately.

name: Easy Guacamole
directions: Place all ingredients in a bowl and blend with hand blender until smooth.
Or mush ingredients well with fork if you prefer your guacamole chunky.
Serve with tortilla chips or as a side to enchiladas or other Mexican dish.

name: Southern Living Salsa
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a medium size bowl and mix well.
Cover and chill.
Serve cold.

name: Yummy Guacamole
directions: Mix all ingredients and chill in frig for a couple of hours.  -- Enjoy!

name: Easy Guacamole
directions: Peel and pit avacados.
Mash avacados and stir all the ingredients together.

name: Avocado Salad
directions: Add avocados and tomatoes to large serving bowl.  In a food processer, mix together the cilantro, onion, and jalapeno. If you do not have a food processor, finely chop with a knife. Add to bowl and stir lightly.  If you stir too much, the salad will turn into a guacamole.  Sprinkle with seasoning and garlic powder, and salt to your individual taste and mix.  Squeeze lemon into mixture and stir.  Enjoy!

name: Easy Corn Salsa
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl.  Mix well and chill for at least 1 hour before serving.  Serve with Tortilla chips or Pita chips.  Refrigerate left overs for up to 3 days.

name: Great Guacamole
directions: Cut the tomato in quarters. Remove seeds and chop finely.
Combine the remaining ingredients, mashing it all together.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Put the seed from the avocado in the bowl with the guacamole (helps keep it from browning).
Serve with tortilla chips or whatever you want.
YUM!

name: Fresh Homemade Salsa
directions: Clear Jalapenos of seeds and stems  (leave seeds if you like a bit!).
Blend Jalapenos and Tomatos until smooth.
Mix in Onions and Cilantro.
Season with Seasoned Salt.
Add avocado, as much as desired.

name: Guacamole: a Basic Recipe
directions: Remove avocados from their skins by slicing entire fruit around the center length-wise and then twisting to separate.
After removing the pit , use a spoon to get the flesh out of the skins and into the bowl.
Add in the jalapeno, red onion and tomato.
Sprinkle with a few generous pinches of sea salt and fresh ground pepper.
Squeeze the juice of one lime over everything.
Using with a large fork or a potato masher, gently mash and mix all of the ingredients just until everything distributed evenly.
Break out the tortilla chips and enjoy!
